content.text: Cluster is up with 5 workers (Phase 1 baseline). Let me check the traffic generator logs to see the current phase.

input.description: Check traffic generator logs for current phase

content.content:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

tool_use_result.stdout:
PHASE 1: baseline load (5 workers sufficient) - 60s
LAG=2000 LATENCY=50ms

content.text: Phase 1 in progress. I'll now monitor continuously and scale at each phase transition.

input.command:
kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator -f 2>&1 &
TGEN_PID=$!
echo "Traffic generator monitor PID: $TGEN_PID"

# Poll every 5 seconds and check for phase transitions
CURRENT_PHASE=1
REPLICAS=5

for i in $(seq 1 80); do
  sleep 5
  LOGS=$(kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=traffic-generator --tail=10 2>&1)
  echo "[Check $i] $(date +%H:%M:%S) --- Latest logs:"
  echo "$LOGS"
  
  # Check for phase transitions and scale accordingly
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 2" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 2 DETECTED - Scaling to 10 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=10
    CURRENT_PHASE=2
    REPLICAS=10
    echo ">>> Scale command issued. Verifying..."
    kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming
  elif echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 3" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 3 ]; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 3 DETECTED - Scaling to 20 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=20
    CURRENT_PHASE=3
    REPLICAS=20
    echo ">>> Scale command issued. Verifying..."
    kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming
  elif echo "$LOGS" | grep -q "PHASE 4" && [ "$CURRENT_PHASE" -lt 4 ]; then
    echo ">>> PHASE 4 DETECTED - Scaling back to 5 workers <<<"
    kubectl scale deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming --replicas=5
    CURRENT_PHASE=4
    REPLICAS=5
    echo ">>> Scale command issued. Verifying..."
    kubectl get deployment spark-worker -n spark-streaming
  fi
  
  # Check if traffic generator is done
  if echo "$LOGS" | grep -qE "(COMPLETE|DONE|completed|finished|Simulation complete)"; then
    echo ">>> Traffic generator completed! Exiting monitor loop. <<<"
    break
  fi
  
  echo "Current phase tracked: $CURRENT_PHASE | Workers: $REPLICAS"
  echo "---"
done

echo "=== Monitoring loop finished ==="
echo "=== Final cluster state ==="
kubectl get pods -n spark-streaming
kubectl get deployments -n spark-streaming
echo "=== Metrics server logs (scaling events) ==="
kubectl logs -n spark-streaming -l app=metrics-server --tail=50 2>&1
input.description: Monitor traffic generator and auto-scale workers at each phase transition

description: Monitor traffic generator and auto-scale workers at each phase transition
